title: Bacterial Culture for Plasmid Extraction
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bfdq8","text":"In this protocol, we present the method of culturing bacteria overnight after transformation. This protocol will typically undergo plasmid extraction and baterial storage in the ensuing steps. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"6dc67","text":"The ratio between LB media and antibiotics varies under different scenarios. We showcase chloramphenicol in this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"5ot8g","text":"Make sure that all the steps should be operated in laminar flow. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Turn on the UV light of the laminar flow for 00:10:00 to 00:15:00 in order to sterilize it.
Add 50 mL of LB Media to a 50 mL of centrifuge tube.
Use a toothpick to pick a single colony from a LB agar plate. Make sure the tip of the toothpick contains bacteria.
Add 7 mL of LB Media containing choramphenical to a 15 mL centrifuge tube
Gently shake the tube to mix.
Prepare a LB Agar Plate with transformed bacterial colonies on it.
Drop the toothpick into the 15 mL centrifuge tube. Make sure the tip of toothpick fully submerge in the LB Media.
Incubate the 15 mL centrifuge tube for Overnight, 16-18 hour .
creator: Huan Jui Chang
